content: Just as the language-learning definition of reflexion frames it as a recursive practice of re-evaluating past language outputs to build more coherent, contextually accurate understanding, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow the structured reflective pedagogy from high school language writing workshops to improve their error correction capabilities. Specifically, LLM self-reflection can adopt the same sequential 'draft writing → peer review → personal reflexion → revised draft' workflow used in ESL writing classes to systematically refine inconsistent reasoning outputs.
